body.title: NE empty → desperation LTM kick in coa.py
body.result: force_push() added to MemorySurfacer; called from coa.py NE-empty branch; surfaces top-5 hot memories by activation_count
body.description:
When NE produces no result in coa.py (~line 237), actively kick MemorySurfacer with a lowered threshold — top-N memories by activation_score with no keyword match required. Currently the NE-empty path only calls escalate_to_channel() then continues, leaving TWM starved until boredom accumulates over 20 min. The fix: add force_push(cortex, top_n=5) to MemorySurfacer in push_sources.py that queries clan.memories ORDER BY activation_score DESC LIMIT top_n (no keyword filter, skip dedup for fresh content), then call it from the coa.py NE-empty branch. Rate-limit: one force_push per NE cycle — guard via a flag on MemorySurfacer that resets after the normal push cadence (300s). This is the proactive 'sensory deprivation search' pattern.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/coa.py (NE-empty branch ~line 237), wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (MemorySurfacer class ~line 148, add force_push method)
**Design rules:** OOP-first (force_push() is a method on MemorySurfacer, not a standalone function); no-sqlite (query clan.memories via existing DB conn pattern)
**Scope boundary:** IN: MemorySurfacer.force_push() new method; coa.py NE-empty caller with per-cycle rate-limit guard. OUT: changes to normal push() cadence; NE result logic; escalation behavior (escalate_to_channel still fires)
**Test plan:** After Igor restart, let NE go empty (or deplete TWM manually). Observe: memory_surfacer entries appear in instance.twm_observations within 1 NE cycle; channel confirms activation rather than silence. No unit test (cognition loop integration only).
